Spring & fall yard cleanups in Guelph

Everything the season left behind — cleared, tidied, and hauled away, so your yard is ready for what’s next.

Get a Free Quote Call or Text
Spring cleanup

Wake your yard up right

  • Rake and remove matted leaves, sticks and winter debris
  • Clean out garden beds and re-define edges
  • First cut of the season at the correct height
  • Haul-away of all waste included

Book early — spring slots in Guelph fill fast once the snow is gone.

Fall cleanup

Put the lawn to bed properly

  • Full leaf cleanup — lawn, beds, and hard surfaces
  • Final cut of the season
  • Beds tidied so spring starts easier
  • All waste removed from your property

A proper fall cleanup prevents snow mould and matted dead patches in April. Your spring self will thank you.

Local detail

We take the waste with us

Guelph’s green cart doesn’t accept grass clippings, and curbside yard-waste pickup only runs on set dates — which is how cleanup bags end up sitting by the garage for weeks. Every Yardex cleanup includes haul-away, so when we leave, the yard is actually done.

Local know-how

What a proper cleanup does for the season ahead

A spring cleanup isn't cosmetic — matted leaves and winter debris smother grass just as it's trying to wake up, and they're the perfect incubator for snow mold and vole damage to linger. Clearing beds and turf early, then following with a first cut, lets the lawn dry, breathe and green up weeks faster.

Fall is the same logic in reverse. Leaves left through winter pack into a wet mat that kills patches by April, and perennials left standing shelter overwintering pests. A final cut at the right height, full leaf removal, and beds put to rest properly is the cheapest insurance your spring lawn can buy.

The most common mistake homeowners make is timing: cleaning up too early in spring compacts soggy soil, and too late in fall means fighting frozen, matted leaves. We watch the conditions across our whole Guelph route and book each cleanup when the ground is actually ready.

Common questions

Frequently asked

When should a spring cleanup happen in Guelph?

Once the lawn is firm enough to walk on without leaving footprints — usually mid-April, but it varies year to year. Working a soggy lawn does more harm than good, so we book by conditions, not the calendar.

What's included in a Yardex cleanup?

Leaf and debris removal from lawn and beds, bed edging tidy-up, first or final cut, and haul-away or bagging of everything we collect. Spring and fall scopes differ slightly — your quote spells it out.

Do you take the debris away?

Yes — everything we clear is bagged for city yard-waste pickup or hauled, your choice at quoting. Nothing gets left in a corner of your yard.

Is a fall cleanup really necessary if I mulch my leaves?

Light leaf cover can be mulched into the lawn and that's healthy. Heavy cover — most mature Guelph streets — is too much and will mat. We'll tell you which side your property falls on.

How short should the final fall cut be?

Slightly lower than summer height, around 2.5 inches — short enough to resist matting and snow mold, tall enough to protect the crown. It's the one time of year we cut below 3 inches.

Can I book cleanup without weekly mowing?

Absolutely. Cleanups are standalone services — though many clients start with a cleanup and stay for the season.
